raw_text:
Staff UX Designer
Zenith Design Systems Inc. — Empowering teams to build human-centered digital experiences at scale

Location

San Francisco, CA (Hybrid - 2 days/week onsite)

Employment Type

Full-time

Compensation

$240,000 - $320,000 USD annually

The Opportunity

We're seeking a Staff UX Designer with 8-12 years of experience to lead strategic design initiatives and provide

technical mentorship across our design and product teams. In this role, you'll drive design excellence, establish best
practices for accessibility and design systems, and collaborate cross-functionally to shape the future of our client
engagements and internal platforms. This is a senior technical leadership position where you'll influence design
strategy, mentor junior designers, and own complex, high-impact projects.

About Zenith Design Systems Inc.

Zenith Design Systems Inc. is a 150-person design infrastructure and UX consulting firm that helps enterprises
build scalable, accessible digital products. Founded in 2015, we've worked with Fortune 500 companies across

fintech, healthcare, and e-commerce to establish design systems, conduct large-scale user research, and establish
design excellence practices. Our mission is to democratize world-class UX across organizations of all sizes.

Day-to-Day

Lead the design vision and strategy for 2-3 major client engagements, owning the end-to-end UX process from

discovery through implementation and measurement

Architect and evolve our internal design system, establishing components, patterns, and accessibility standards
(WCAG 2.1 AAA compliance) that scale across client projects

Conduct and mentor others on advanced user research methodologies including moderated/unmoderated
usability testing, diary studies, and generative research using tools like UserTesting and Maze

Design high-fidelity prototypes in Figma that demonstrate complex interactions and state management,
establishing reusable component libraries and design tokens

Establish and enforce accessibility guidelines across all design work, performing WCAG audits, creating

accessible design specifications, and training team members on inclusive design practices

Mentor 3-4 mid-level UX designers and researchers, providing technical feedback on design work, design

system implementations, and research findings

Partner with our Head of Design to develop design hiring criteria, conduct technical interviews, and build team
capability in prototyping, design systems, and accessibility

Document design decisions, patterns, and principles in our internal knowledge base; present findings and
recommendations to clients and internal stakeholders

 Contribute to thought leadership through internal workshops, conference speaking opportunities, and published
content on design systems and accessibility best practices

You Have

8-12 years of professional UX/product design experience, with at least 3+ years in a senior or staff-level role

Expert proficiency in Figma, including component architecture, design tokens, variants, and collaborative design
workflows

Demonstrated expertise in design systems work, including component specification, documentation, and

governance across design and engineering teams

Strong background in user research methodologies and usability testing; experience with tools like UserTesting,
Maze, or similar platforms

Proven ability to design for accessibility (WCAG compliance) and mentor others on inclusive design principles

Track record of mentoring junior designers and contributing to team growth and capability building

Excellent communication and presentation skills, with ability to influence stakeholders and articulate design
rationale to non-design audiences

Bonus

Experience with advanced prototyping tools such as Framer, Principle, or Sketch prototyping

Background in design system implementation and integration with Storybook or similar component
documentation platforms

Experience conducting or managing large-scale user research programs across multiple product lines or clients

Familiarity with accessibility testing tools such as Axe, NVDA, or JAWS screen readers

Published writing, conference talks, or contributions to the design community on topics like design systems,
accessibility, or design operations

Our Stack

Figma (component libraries, design systems, design tokens)

UserTesting and Maze (moderated and unmoderated usability testing)

WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards and audit methodologies

Prototyping tools (Framer or Principle for high-ﬁdelity interaction design)

Storybook (component documentation and design system integration)   Axe and other accessibility auditing tools

Design operations tools (Miro for collaborative research and brainstorming)

Analytics tools (Mixpanel or Amplitude) for measuring design impact

Jira and Asana (cross-functional collaboration with engineering and product)

What We Offer

Competitive PTO: 25 days annually, plus 10 company holidays and unlimited sick days

Comprehensive health coverage: medical, dental, vision with 100% employer premium coverage for employees

 Professional development stipend: $3,000/year for conferences, courses, and design tools

$1.2M term life insurance, disability coverage, and 401(k) matching up to 6%

Remote-first flexibility: work hybrid (2 days/week onsite) or fully remote with team agreement

Equity: 0.5-1.5% stock options with 4-year vest

Wellness program: monthly gym stipend ($100), mental health support (Headspace), and annual wellness retreat

Parental leave: 16 weeks paid for all parents, plus flexible return-to-work arrangements

Apply

Submit your application through our careers page at zenith-design.com/careers. Please include: (1) resume, (2)
portfolio with 2-3 case studies demonstrating your design system, accessibility, or mentorship work, (3) a 1-page

statement on your approach to design leadership and accessibility. Applications reviewed on a rolling basis. We are
committed to building a diverse and inclusive team and encourage candidates from underrepresented backgrounds
in design to apply.
